engineGuide.h2: BMW X3 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the BMW X3 across all generations (E83, F25, G01), with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Replaced the M47 in 2007, bringing aluminium construction and common-rail injection - but also the infamous rear-mounted timing chain that would define X3 replacement demand for a decade.
compatibleVariants: E83 2.0d, E83 2.0d M Sport, F25 sDrive18d, F25 sDrive18d M Sport, F25 xDrive20d, F25 xDrive20d M Sport, F25 xDrive20d xLine
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch and failure - affects approximately 15-20% of units; chain can snap causing catastrophic engine damage
Injector failures causing rough idling and poor fuel economy
Oil leaks from various seals and gaskets
entries.cta: Get quotes for N47D20 BMW X3 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Diesel (Updated)
entries.history: A revised version of the N47 introduced in late F25 production, featuring timing chain tensioner updates but retaining the same fundamental design.
compatibleVariants: F25 xDrive20d, F25 xDrive20d M Sport, F25 xDrive20d xLine (late production)
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure leading to chain stretch
EGR valve clogging with carbon deposits
DPF regeneration issues on short journeys
entries.cta: Get quotes for N47D20C BMW X3 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: Launched in 2014 as the N47’s successor, relocating the timing chain to the front of the engine for improved durability and serviceability. Generally regarded as a strong, dependable unit capable of high mileage.
compatibleVariants: F25 xDrive20d (late), G01 xDrive20d, G01 xDrive20d M Sport, G01 xDrive20d xLine
commonFailures: EGR valve and cooler failures (subject to recalls on some vehicles)
DPF clogging, especially on vehicles driven mostly in urban conditions
Timing chain wear at very high mileage (far less frequent than N47)
entries.cta: Get quotes for B47D20 BMW X3 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: The "bulletproof" straight-six diesel that built BMW’s diesel reputation in the UK. Known for smooth power delivery, strong torque, and exceptional longevity when properly maintained.
compatibleVariants: E83 3.0d, E83 3.0d M Sport, E83 xDrive30d
commonFailures: Injector failure causing rough idle and smoky exhaust
Glow plug failure, particularly on higher-mileage units
Turbocharger wastegate wear affecting boost delivery
entries.cta: Get quotes for M57D30 BMW X3 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Diesel
entries.history: The twin-turbocharged successor to the M57, offering significantly more power while maintaining reasonable fuel economy. Found across F25 and early G01 X3 ranges.
compatibleVariants: F25 xDrive30d, F25 xDrive30d M Sport, F25 xDrive30d xLine, F25 xDrive35d, F25 xDrive35d M Sport, G01 xDrive30d, G01 xDrive30d M Sport
commonFailures: Timing chain issues at higher mileages (120k+ miles)
Exhaust back pressure sensor failure (P143E code)
Turbocharger bearing wear on higher-output variants
entries.cta: Get quotes for N57D30 BMW X3 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The naturally aspirated straight-six that powered early E83 X3s. Part of BMW’s legendary M54 family, known for smooth power delivery and the classic six-cylinder soundtrack.
commonFailures: CCV (crankcase ventilation) system failure causing oil consumption
Cooling system leaks from expansion tank and hoses
VANOS seal deterioration affecting low-end power
entries.cta: Get quotes for M54B25 BMW X3 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The last naturally aspirated straight-six sold in the US market, featuring a lightweight magnesium/aluminium block and electric water pump. Defines the E83 X3 3.0si.
commonFailures: Electric water pump failure (can fail without warning)
Valve cover gasket oil leaks
DISA flap failure causing rattling and power loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for N52B30 BMW X3 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: BMW’s first mass-produced four-cylinder turbocharged petrol engine, introduced to the F25 X3 as part of the EfficientDynamics push. Twin-scroll turbo provides responsive power delivery.
compatibleVariants: F25 xDrive20i, F25 xDrive20i M Sport, F25 xDrive28i, F25 xDrive28i M Sport
commonFailures: Timing chain guide failure (similar vulnerability to N47 diesel)
High-pressure fuel pump failure
Camshaft bearing ledge wear
entries.cta: Get quotes for N20B20 BMW X3 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: BMW’s first mass-produced turbocharged straight-six, featuring a single twin-scroll turbo rather than the twin-turbo setup of the N54. Renowned for responsive power and reliability.
compatibleVariants: F25 xDrive35i, F25 xDrive35i M Sport
commonFailures: Electric water pump failure
Oil filter housing gasket leaks
VANOS solenoid issues
entries.cta: Get quotes for N55B30 BMW X3 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: Part of BMW’s modular B-series engine family, replacing the N20. Features improved thermal management, reduced friction, and greater tuning potential. Fitted to G01 X3 from launch.
compatibleVariants: G01 xDrive20i, G01 xDrive20i M Sport, G01 xDrive20i xLine, G01 xDrive30i, G01 xDrive30i M Sport
commonFailures: Injector seal leaks
Coolant loss from various connections
PCV system failure on higher-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for B48B20 BMW X3 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The legendary straight-six successor to the N55, part of BMW’s modular B-series family. Featured in the X3 M40i, delivering 355bhp and exceptional refinement. Regularly praised as one of BMW’s best modern engines.
compatibleVariants: G01 X3 M40i, G01 X3 M40i M Sport
commonFailures: Coolant leak from the coolant transfer pipe (known weak point)
Oil filter housing failure
PCV diaphragm failure on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for B58B30 BMW X3 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0 Litre Petrol
entries.history: BMW M GmbH’s most powerful straight-six ever produced, developed specifically for X3 M and X4 M. Features a closed-deck block, twin mono-scroll turbos, and 350 bar fuel injection.
compatibleVariants: G01 X3 M, G01 X3 M Competition
commonFailures: Oil consumption on high-performance driving cycles
Crank hub concerns (shared with S55 engine family)
Cooling system demands under track use
entries.cta: Get quotes for S58B30 BMW X3 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: BMW X3 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The BMW X3’s reputation in the UK is heavily shaped by its diesel engines, particularly the N47 2.0-litre unit fitted to E83 and early F25 models, where timing chain failure is the single biggest reason owners seek replacement. The improved B47 diesel and the straight-six M57/N57 family have their own failure patterns - but every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the most common X3 engine failures, what genuine repair costs look like, and when a rebuilt engine is the smarter financial decision.

problems.group: BMW X3 N47 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: BMW X3 N47 Timing Chain Failure - Why 2.0 Diesel Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: BMW X3 E83 2.0d, F25 sDrive18d, F25 xDrive20d (2004-2015, N47D20 & N47D20C)
problems.rootCause: The timing chain is mounted at the rear of the engine, making it difficult to lubricate adequately. Plastic chain guides become brittle with heat cycling, fracture, and allow the chain to stretch or snap.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Chain Replacement - Catch it early
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides, and sprockets. Oil change.
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles. Only viable if engine has no rattles and chain has not jumped timing.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Post-Failure Chain & Sprockets - Chain stretched but not snapped
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full chain kit plus replacement sprockets, oil pump chain, sump clean.
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-60k miles. Risk remains of existing crank/cam damage.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Chain snapped or engine seized
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt N47 engine with upgraded timing chain components, bench tested, fresh oil and filters.
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-120k miles. Full warranty. Only sensible option after catastrophic failure.

problems.recommendation: If your X3’s N47 is rattling on start-up but still drives normally, the preventative chain replacement is excellent value through a specialist - around £1,600-£2,200, less than half the dealer cost. Once the chain has snapped, replacement is the only realistic route. On an older E83 X3 worth £2,000-£4,000, a £5,000+ dealer repair makes no sense; a specialist-fitted rebuilt engine at £3,500-£5,000 is the pragmatic choice.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N47 engine prices for your BMW X3

problems.h4: BMW X3 EGR Cooler Failure - Coolant Loss & Hydrolock Risk
problems.affectedModels: BMW X3 F25 xDrive20d (N47), G01 xDrive20d (B47) (2011-2020, N47D20C & B47D20)
problems.rootCause: The EGR cooler develops internal cracks, allowing coolant to leak into the exhaust gas stream. In severe cases, coolant enters the combustion chambers, causing hydraulic lock (hydrolock) and bent connecting rods.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - EGR Cooler Replacement - Coolant loss, no engine damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler, flush cooling system, replace lost coolant, reset adaptations.
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles. Full fix if caught before coolant reaches cylinders.

repairOptions.tier: Major - Cooler + Top End Overhaul - Coolant ingress, rough running
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR cooler replacement plus cylinder head removal, inspection, valve cleaning, new head gasket.
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles. Viable if no bent rods confirmed.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Hydrolock confirmed
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt B47 or N47 engine with revised EGR cooler, full flush, warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-120k miles. Required if compression loss or rod damage present.

problems.recommendation: EGR cooler failure is insidious - white smoke and coolant loss are early warnings. At specialist prices, a £500-£800 cooler replacement is cheap insurance. If you’ve already seen coolant loss and the engine runs rough, pay for a borescope inspection before committing to a full rebuild. Hydrolock bends rods; once confirmed, a rebuilt engine is the only safe path.
problems.cta: Get B47 and N47 EGR repair quotes for your X3

problems.group: BMW X3 DPF & P143E Exhaust Temperature Sensor Failure
problems.h4: BMW X3 DPF & P143E Exhaust Temperature Sensor Failure - Reduced Power & Regeneration Blocked
problems.affectedModels: BMW X3 F25 xDrive20d, xDrive30d, xDrive35d, G01 xDrive20d (2010-2020, N47D20, N57D30, B47D20)
problems.rootCause: The exhaust temperature sensor (P143E code) fails, preventing the DPF from performing active regenerations. The DPF clogs, exhaust backpressure rises, and the ECU limits power to protect the engine.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Sensor Replacement - P143E code, regeneration still possible
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace failed exhaust temperature sensor, reset adaptations, force manual regeneration.
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-60k miles. Full solution if DPF is not yet clogged.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Sensor + DPF Clean - P143E plus clogged DPF
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace sensor plus off-car DPF thermal cleaning or chemical flush.
repairOptions.longevity: 30k-50k miles. Viable if DPF not cracked or melted.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - DPF meltdown, turbo damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt engine plus new DPF, sensor, oil flush. Required if turbo seals failed.
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-120k miles. Catastrophic scenario - clogged DPF overheats and damages turbo.

problems.recommendation: The P143E code is often ignored until the X3 enters limp mode. A £200-£350 sensor replacement at a specialist is all most cars need. However, if you’ve been driving with the warning light for months, the DPF will likely be clogged. Budget for a DPF clean (£500-£800) as well. Only proceed to engine replacement if the overheat has damaged turbo seals - confirmed by excessive oil consumption or smoke.
problems.cta: Compare X3 diesel engine replacement prices - P143E diagnosis

problems.group: BMW X3 N57 Diesel Injector Failure
problems.h4: BMW X3 N57 Diesel Injector Failure - Rough Idle, Smoke & Potential Piston Damage
problems.affectedModels: BMW X3 F25 xDrive30d, xDrive35d, G01 xDrive30d (2010-2019, N57D30)
problems.rootCause: Piezo injectors on the N57 engine develop internal leakage or poor spray patterns. Unburned fuel washes cylinder walls, dilutes oil, and can hydrolock or burn a piston in extreme cases.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Single Injector Replacement - One injector failed
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnose failed injector, replace with OEM or refurbished unit, code to ECU.
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-60k miles. Fine for isolated failure on otherwise healthy engine.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Set Replacement - Multiple failures, preventative
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all six injectors, copper washers, coding, fuel filter, oil change.
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k miles. Recommended if one fails beyond 100k miles.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Piston or bore damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt N57 engine with new injectors, timing chain, full warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-120k miles. Injector failure that melts a piston leaves no alternative.

problems.recommendation: N57 injector failure is progressive. A single failed injector at a specialist costs £450-£700 and gets you back on the road. However, if your X3 is over 100k miles and one injector has failed, we recommend replacing all six preventatively - the labour to access them is the same, and you’ll avoid repeated garage visits. If the engine is already misfiring badly and misfuel codes appear, pay for a compression test before any repair. Piston damage = replacement.
problems.cta: Get N57 injector replacement quotes for your X3 xDrive30d

problems.group: BMW X3 M57 & N57 Turbocharger Failure
problems.h4: BMW X3 M57 & N57 Turbocharger Failure - Loss of Boost & Whining Noise
problems.affectedModels: BMW X3 E83 3.0d, F25 xDrive30d, xDrive35d (2003-2017, M57D30 & N57D30)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger bearings wear over high mileage, allowing the compressor wheel to contact the housing. On variable geometry turbos, the unison ring seizes due to carbon build-up.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Cartridge Replacement - Bearing wear, early whine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace CHRA cartridge, balance, new gaskets, oil flush.
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles. Viable if no debris in oil system.

repairOptions.tier: Major - Reconditioned Turbo Unit - Significant shaft play
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace with fully reconditioned turbo unit, new oil feed pipes, full flush.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Turbo disintegrated into engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt engine with new turbo, oil cooler, full oil system flush.
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-120k miles. Required if turbo fragments have circulated.

problems.recommendation: On the M57, turbo failure is rarely an engine-killer if caught early. The whine or loss of boost at 120k+ miles means replace the turbo cartridge or fit a recon unit - £1,000-£2,000 at a specialist. However, if the turbo has disintegrated and sent metal through the oil system, only a full rebuilt engine guarantees reliability. If you see blue smoke and hear grinding, stop driving immediately and arrange a specialist inspection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt X3 3.0d engine prices - M57 & N57 specialists

problems.group: BMW X3 B58 M40i Coolant Leak
problems.h4: BMW X3 B58 M40i Coolant Leak - Engine Overheating Risk
problems.rootCause: The coolant transfer pipe (made of plastic) at the front of the B58 engine becomes brittle and cracks, causing sudden coolant loss. The engine management system reduces power, but continued driving causes overheating and head gasket failure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Coolant Transfer Pipe Replacement - Coolant leak, no overheating
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace plastic coolant transfer pipe with updated version, bleed cooling system.
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix with revised pipe. Labour-intensive due to intake removal.

repairOptions.tier: Major - Pipe + Head Gasket - Overheating occurred
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Pipe replacement plus cylinder head gasket, head skim, pressure test.
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles. Viable if head not warped.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Head warped or cracked
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt B58 engine with revised cooling components, full warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-120k miles. Required after severe overheating event.

problems.recommendation: B58 coolant leaks are the single most discussed issue on X3 M40i forums. If you smell coolant or see the low coolant warning, act immediately. The preventative pipe replacement costs £700-£1,100 at a specialist - a small price on a £25,000+ car. Ignore it, and a £5,000-£7,500 rebuilt engine becomes the only fix. Check your coolant level monthly.
problems.cta: Get B58 engine replacement quotes for X3 M40i specialists

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your BMW X3
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your X3 suffers from the N47 timing chain rattle, N57 injector failure, B58 coolant leak, or DPF-related issues, the cost of a main dealer repair can easily exceed the value of an older car. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK BMW X3 specialists who supply rebuilt, reconditioned, and used engines - each with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and upgraded components addressing the original failure point. Compare prices, choose supply & fit, and get your X3 back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare BMW X3 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific BMW X3 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: BMW X3 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the BMW X3 across E83, F25 and G01 generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years, power outputs and common failure notes.

items.title: BMW X3 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre diesel is the backbone of the X3 range in the UK, accounting for the majority of sales across all three generations. From the problematic N47 in early E83 and F25 models to the significantly improved B47 in later F25 and G01 cars, this displacement defines X3 engine replacement demand. Common-rail injection and turbocharging deliver strong economy, but the N47's rear-mounted timing chain remains the single biggest failure point in X3 history.
commonFailurePoints: guide fracture on N47 units; EGR cooler failure on B47
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X3 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: BMW X3 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The straight-six 3.0-litre diesel delivers effortless torque and genuine long-distance refinement, making it the choice for high-mileage drivers and towing users. Across E83, F25 and early G01 models, this displacement uses the M57 (legendary for durability) and later N57 (more power, higher failure risk). The six-cylinder units carry a higher purchase price but often outlast the four-cylinder diesels when properly maintained.
commonFailurePoints: Injector failure on N57; turbocharger bearing wear at high mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X3 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: BMW X3 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Turbocharged 2.0-litre petrol power arrived with the F25 generation, offering a lighter, more fuel-efficient alternative to the six-cylinder petrols. The N20B20 brought twin-scroll turbo technology to the X3, though it shared timing chain vulnerabilities with its N47 diesel cousin. The later B48B20 in G01 models is significantly more reliable, making it the preferred choice for X3 xDrive20i and xDrive30i buyers.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain guide wear on N20; injector seal leaks on B48
items.cta: Compare BMW X3 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW X3 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 2.5-litre straight-six powered early E83 X3s before BMW moved to four-cylinder turbo units. Part of the revered M54 family, this engine offers smooth power delivery and the classic six-cylinder soundtrack, though it lacks the torque of modern turbocharged alternatives. Only available in the first-generation E83 X3 2.5i, these units are now relatively rare but sought after by enthusiasts.
commonFailurePoints: CCV system failure; cooling system leaks; VANOS seal deterioration
items.cta: Compare BMW X3 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW X3 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre petrol straight-six represents the pinnacle of non-M X3 petrol engineering across three generations. From the naturally aspirated M54 (E83 3.0i) and N52 (E83 3.0si) to the turbocharged N55 (F25 xDrive35i) and the exceptional B58 (G01 X3 M40i), this displacement delivers power, refinement and the signature straight-six character. The B58 in particular is widely regarded as one of BMW's finest modern engines.
commonFailurePoints: Electric water pump failure (N52/N55); coolant transfer pipe leak (B58)
items.cta: Compare BMW X3 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW X3 3.0L Competition Petrol Engines
items.description: The S58 engine fitted to the X3 M and X3 M Competition is BMW M GmbH's most powerful straight-six, sharing architecture with the B58 but featuring a closed-deck block, forged internals and twin mono-scroll turbos. Producing up to 510 hp in Competition form, this engine transforms the X3 into a genuine performance SUV. Replacement costs reflect its specialist nature, with rebuilt units commanding a significant premium over standard B58 engines.
commonFailurePoints: Oil consumption under hard use; crank hub concerns on tuned examples
items.cta: Enquire about BMW X3 3.0L competition petrol engine availability

items.title: BMW X3 2.0L Plug-In Hybrid Engines
items.description: The xDrive30e introduced a plug-in hybrid powertrain to the G01 X3 range, combining a B48B20 2.0-litre petrol engine with an electric motor for a combined 292 hp. The system allows up to 30 miles of electric-only range, making it attractive for company car drivers. While the combustion engine itself is reliable, the hybrid system adds complexity and specialised replacement considerations. Not sure which engine size your BMW X3 needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK BMW X3 specialists.
commonFailurePoints: High-voltage battery degradation; electric motor drivetrain issues
items.cta: Enquire about BMW X3 2.0L plug-in hybrid engine availability

fuelTypes.h2: BMW X3 Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your BMW X3 engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early E83 diesels to the latest G01 performance petrols.

items.title: BMW X3 Diesel Engines
items.description: The UK X3 market is dominated by diesel engines across all three generations, with the 2.0-litre N47 accounting for the highest replacement volume due to timing chain failures.
items.descriptor: The UK X3 market is dominated by diesel engines across all three generations, with the 2.0-litre N47 accounting for the highest replacement volume due to timing chain failures.
families: N47D20 - The most replaced X3 engine; rear timing chain fails 60k-120k miles (E83, early F25)
N47D20C - Revised version with updated tensioner but same fundamental vulnerability (late F25)
B47D20 - Improved successor with front timing chain; fewer failures but EGR cooler issues (late F25, G01)
M57D30 - "Bulletproof" straight-six; injector and glow plug failures at high mileage (E83 3.0d)
N57D30 - Twin-turbo straight-six; injector and timing chain concerns (F25 xDrive30d/35d, early G01)
knownFor: Excellent motorway economy and long-distance refinement
N47 timing chain failure - the #1 replacement driver in X3 history
High replacement demand on xDrive20d models across F25 generation
M57 3.0d regarded as one of BMW's most durable diesel engines
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X3 Diesel replacement engines

items.title: BMW X3 Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol X3s are less common in the UK than diesels, but the B58-powered M40i has created strong replacement demand among performance SUV owners.
items.descriptor: Petrol X3s are less common in the UK than diesels, but the B58-powered M40i has created strong replacement demand among performance SUV owners.
families: M54B25 - Naturally aspirated straight-six; smooth but aging (E83 2.5i)
N52B30 - Last naturally aspirated straight-six; electric water pump fails (E83 3.0si)
N20B20 - Turbo four-cylinder; timing chain guide issues similar to N47 (F25 xDrive20i/28i)
N55B30 - Turbo straight-six; water pump and oil leaks (F25 xDrive35i)
B48B20 - Modular turbo four; reliable with occasional injector seal leaks (G01 xDrive20i/30i)
B58B30 - Legendary turbo straight-six; coolant transfer pipe weakness (G01 M40i)
S58B30 - M Division twin-turbo; 500hp+, oil consumption under hard use (G01 X3 M)
knownFor: Lower UK sales volume than diesel - fewer replacement enquiries
B58 engine widely praised for reliability and power delivery
M40i owners pay premium for specialist B58 rebuilt units
Naturally aspirated straight-sixes offer classic BMW character
items.cta: Compare BMW X3 Petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW X3 Plug-In Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: The xDrive30e joined the G01 X3 range in 2019, combining a B48 petrol engine with an electric motor for lower benefit-in-kind tax and urban electric running.
items.descriptor: The xDrive30e joined the G01 X3 range in 2019, combining a B48 petrol engine with an electric motor for lower benefit-in-kind tax and urban electric running.
families: B48B20 (PHEV variant) - 2.0-litre turbo petrol paired with electric motor
Electric motor provides up to 30 miles of zero-emission urban driving
Petrol engine automatically engages for longer journeys or heavy acceleration
Battery charges via regenerative braking or external plug-in source
foundIn: G01 xDrive30e (2019-2024), G01 xDrive30e M Sport (2019-2024)
importantNotes: Combustion engine block is standard B48 - replacement follows same process
High-voltage battery inspection recommended during engine replacement
Hybrid system adds complexity - use specialists familiar with BMW PHEV
Lower replacement volume than diesel, but growing as G01 ages
items.cta: Enquire about BMW X3 Plug-In Hybrid engine availability

items.title: BMW X3 Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: Late G01 X3 diesel and petrol models received 48-volt mild hybrid assistance for improved efficiency and reduced turbo lag.
items.descriptor: Late G01 X3 diesel and petrol models received 48-volt mild hybrid assistance for improved efficiency and reduced turbo lag.
families: B47D20 (with MHEV) - 2.0-litre diesel with belt-driven starter-generator
B58B30 (with MHEV) - 3.0-litre petrol with integrated starter-generator
48-volt starter-generator recovers braking energy and assists acceleration
Enables coasting with engine off at motorway speeds
No electric-only driving - purely assistance for efficiency and response
importantNotes: MHEV components are separate from the engine block itself
Engine replacement requires correct coding of the starter-generator
Limited specialist network - confirm MHEV experience before booking
Overall replacement volume remains very low due to vehicle age

Built on E46 3 Series platform with xDrive AWD standard on most markets
5-speed automatic transmission standard; manual optional
Plastic body cladding and firm ride criticised by press
knownFor: First compact SAV from BMW - created the segment
M57 diesel is robust and long-lived with proper maintenance
M54 petrol engines smooth but ageing - cooling system weak points
Harsh ride quality and cheap interior plastics drew criticism

N52 naturally-aspirated straight-six replaces M54 petrol
N47 2.0-litre diesel joins the range for European markets
6-speed automatic transmission upgrades from 5-speed
knownFor: N47 diesel introduced - but rear timing chain becomes major failure point
M57 remains the bulletproof choice for high-mileage owners
N52 electric water pump fails without warning - common replacement driver
Last X3 generation sold before US plant production moved

faq.h2: BMW X3 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is the N47 timing chain failure on the BMW X3 really that bad?
items.answer: Yes. The N47 diesel’s rear-mounted timing chain is the single biggest reason X3 engines fail in the UK. Chain guides become brittle, snap, and the engine destroys itself - often without much warning.
keyPoints: Failure typically occurs between 60,000 and 120,000 miles
Early symptom: metallic rattling from the engine on cold start-up
Once the chain snaps, pistons hit valves - engine is usually scrap
Preventative chain replacement costs £1,600-£2,200 at a specialist
Ignoring the rattle leads to a £4,000-£6,000 replacement bill
items.cta: Compare rebuilt N47 engine prices for your BMW X3

items.question: How much does a BMW X3 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Between £3,500 and £6,500 fitted, depending on engine code and condition. A rebuilt 2.0-litre N47 costs £3,500-£5,000 supply and fit. The 3.0-litre N57 runs £4,500-£6,000 .
keyPoints: Rebuilt N47D20 (2.0 diesel): £3,500-£5,000 fitted
Rebuilt N57D30 (3.0 diesel): £4,500-£6,000 fitted
Supply-only prices are £1,200-£2,500 cheaper
Used engines cost £800-£2,500 but carry higher risk
All figures exclude diagnostics and ancillaries
items.cta: Get instant BMW X3 engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Can you fit a B47 engine into an N47 BMW X3?
items.answer: Not directly. The B47 diesel shares the same 2.0-litre displacement but requires different engine mounts, wiring harnesses, ECU coding, and cooling system components. Most specialists won't attempt the conversion.
keyPoints: Engine mounts and subframe differ between generations
ECU and wiring harness are incompatible without extensive modification
Some owners have succeeded with complete donor vehicle parts
Labour costs typically exceed the value of an older X3
Most specialists recommend a rebuilt N47 with upgraded chain instead
items.cta: Enquire about B47 compatibility for your X3

items.question: What is the most reliable engine in a BMW X3?
items.answer: The M57 3.0-litre straight-six diesel (E83, 2003-2010) is widely regarded as BMW’s most durable X3 engine. For newer models, the B58 petrol in the M40i has an excellent reputation, and the B47 diesel (post-2014) is far better than the N47 it replaced .
keyPoints: M57D30 - bulletproof, capable of 200k+ miles with maintenance
B58B30 (M40i) - modern straight-six, very reliable
B47D20 (2014+) - front timing chain, much improved over N47
N47 - avoid unless chain has been upgraded preventatively
N57 - good but watch for injector failures
items.cta: Find a reconditioned BMW X3 engine with 12-month warranty

items.question: What causes the BMW X3 diesel to lose power and smoke?
items.answer: A blocked Diesel Particulate Filter (DPF) is the most common cause. Frequent short trips prevent the DPF from regenerating, causing soot buildup, reduced power, and black smoke on acceleration .
keyPoints: DPF needs regular long runs to self-clean (regenerate)
Short city driving clogs the filter - common on X3 diesels
Forced regeneration may work - but sometimes the DPF is beyond saving
P143E exhaust temperature sensor failure also blocks regeneration
Ignoring it can damage the turbo and require full engine replacement
items.cta: Compare X3 DPF repair and engine replacement costs

items.question: What oil does a BMW X3 diesel engine need?
items.answer: BMW Longlife-04 approved 5W-30 fully synthetic oil is required for all X3 diesel engines (N47, B47, M57, N57). Using the wrong oil accelerates timing chain wear .
keyPoints: Specification: BMW Longlife-04 (LL04) - mandatory for diesel
Viscosity: 5W-30 is the standard grade
2.0-litre diesel capacity: approx 5.2-5.5 litres
3.0-litre diesel capacity: approx 6.5-7.5 litres
Annual or 12,000-mile changes essential for chain longevity
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X3 engine specialists near you

items.question: Is the BMW X3 xDrive20d (B47) a good engine?
items.answer: Yes, the B47 diesel (2014 onwards) is a major improvement over the N47. BMW relocated the timing chain to the front, making it serviceable and far less prone to catastrophic failure. It’s now considered a dependable, high-mileage unit .
keyPoints: Front-mounted timing chain - accessible and more durable
EGR cooler failures are the main concern (subject to recalls)
190hp version delivers strong economy and performance
Regularly sees 150,000+ miles with proper maintenance
Avoid early 2014 units; late 2014 onwards is safer
items.cta: Compare rebuilt B47 engine prices for your X3 xDrive20d

items.question: What causes EGR cooler failure on BMW X3 diesels?
items.answer: The EGR cooler develops internal cracks, allowing coolant to mix with diesel soot. In rare cases, this can cause smouldering particles, intake manifold melting, and even engine fires. A major BMW recall covers many X3 models .
keyPoints: Affects N47 and B47 diesel X3s from 2014-2020
Symptoms: coolant loss, white smoke, burning smell
Internal coolant leak can hydrolock the engine
BMW recall replaces cooler and intake manifold
If your X3 hasn't had the recall, book it immediately
items.cta: Check your X3's EGR recall status and compare replacement options

items.question: How long does a BMW X3 engine last?
items.answer: Well-maintained M57 and B47 diesel X3s regularly exceed 200,000 miles. N47 engines often fail between 80,000-120,000 miles unless the timing chain is replaced preventatively. Petrol B58 engines are good for 150,000+ miles .
keyPoints: M57 3.0d: 200,000+ miles - legendary durability
B47 2.0d: 150,000-200,000 miles with proper oil changes
N47 2.0d: 80,000-120,000 miles - chain is the limit
B58 petrol: 150,000+ miles - one of BMW's best
Regular LL04 oil changes are the single most important factor
items.cta: Find a high-mileage BMW X3 engine specialist

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on an older BMW X3?
items.answer: It depends. On an E83 X3 (pre-2010) worth £2,000-£4,000, a £5,000 dealer replacement rarely makes sense. But a specialist-fitted rebuilt engine at £3,500-£4,000 can give you another 80,000+ miles - often better value than scrapping and buying another unknown used car.
keyPoints: E83 X3 (2003-2010): borderline - specialist rebuild only
F25 X3 (2011-2017): usually worth replacing if body and transmission are sound
G01 X3 (2017+): absolutely worth replacing - high residual value
Specialist prices are 30-40% less than main dealers
A rebuilt engine with upgraded chain outlasts a used unknown unit
